project.name: sirsi-pantheon
project.description: Sirsi Pantheon — infrastructure intelligence CLI and agent suite for developers and operations teams; scans, detects, and remediates from one laptop to a 256-node fleet using 81 rules, zero config, zero telemetry. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: service.go
nodes.summary: Builds and installs the macOS LaunchAgent that runs the router daemon, including stable binary resolution, plist rendering, and launchctl invocation.
nodes.languageNotes: Hand-rolled XML plist generation with custom xmlEscape/xmlUnescape; intentionally avoids encoding/xml to keep plist output stable.

nodes.name: ServiceOptions
nodes.summary: Struct describing repo/binary paths, label, plist location, and log destinations for the router launch agent.

nodes.name: DefaultServiceOptions
nodes.summary: Returns repo-specific default ServiceOptions with derived label, plist path, and log file destinations under the user's HOME.

nodes.name: ResolveStableBinary
nodes.summary: Resolves a stable sirsi binary path, building one via go build when the candidate appears to be a transient go-run binary.

nodes.name: RenderLaunchAgentPlist
nodes.summary: Renders the LaunchAgent plist XML for the router daemon with KeepAlive, RunAtLoad, and standard out/err redirection.

nodes.name: LaunchAgentProgram
nodes.summary: Parses an installed LaunchAgent plist and returns the configured program path, used to detect drift between rendered and installed daemons.

nodes.name: InstallLaunchAgent
nodes.summary: Writes the rendered LaunchAgent plist to disk, creating parent directories for plist and logs as needed.

nodes.name: Launchctl
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper that invokes the launchctl CLI with the given arguments and surfaces combined stdout/stderr in error messages.

nodes.name: service_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for the LaunchAgent service helpers: plist rendering, repo-specific defaults, transient go-run detection, and program-path readback.

nodes.name: smoke.go
nodes.summary: End-to-end smoke harness that drives a Claude/Codex agent pair through a router round-trip, seeding inbox items and verifying protocol side effects.

nodes.name: SmokeOptions
nodes.summary: Options struct controlling smoke run mode, dry-run flag, agent pair selection, output sink, and timeout.

nodes.name: SmokeResult
nodes.summary: Result record produced per agent probe with pass/fail status, detail message, and elapsed wall time.

nodes.name: RunSmoke
nodes.summary: Top-level entry that either runs a paired agent smoke or probes each agent individually, managing the smoke artifact directory.

nodes.name: runAgentPairSmoke
nodes.summary: Drives the full paired-agent smoke flow: snapshots router state, seeds an addressed inbox item, launches the agent under timeout, and verifies the protocol round-trip.

nodes.name: probeAgent
nodes.summary: Probes a single agent CLI under a constrained timeout, capturing exit status and elapsed time for the smoke report.

nodes.name: verifyProtocolSideEffects
nodes.summary: Verifies that the smoke run produced the expected router protocol side effects: inbox ack, lastRead advance, and artifact persistence.

nodes.name: smoke_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests that exercise smoke dry-run paths to ensure they neither mutate the router state nor create the smoke artifact directory.

nodes.name: threads.go
nodes.summary: Persistent thread registry that tracks active agent/surface threads with heartbeat timestamps and supports staleness pruning.

nodes.name: Thread
nodes.summary: Record representing one router thread, including ID, agent, surface, status, and heartbeat timestamps.

nodes.name: ThreadRegistry
nodes.summary: Container struct wrapping the persisted slice of threads with helper methods for find and prune.

nodes.name: LoadThreadRegistry
nodes.summary: Loads the thread registry JSON from disk, returning an empty registry when the file is absent.

nodes.name: SaveThreadRegistry
nodes.summary: Atomically writes the thread registry to disk using a temp file and rename to avoid torn writes.

nodes.name: RegisterThread
nodes.summary: Registers a new thread with required agent and surface fields, assigning a fresh ID and initial heartbeat.

nodes.name: Heartbeat
nodes.summary: Updates the heartbeat timestamp for an existing thread, returning an error when the thread is unknown.

nodes.name: CloseThread
nodes.summary: Marks a thread as closed with a timestamp so it no longer appears in active sweeps.

nodes.name: PruneClosed
nodes.summary: Drops closed threads older than the configured retention window from the registry.

nodes.name: SortedThreads
nodes.summary: Returns threads sorted newest-first by last heartbeat for stable display in CLI listings.

nodes.name: threads_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests covering thread registry lifecycle: registration, heartbeat, close, staleness detection, sort order, and pruning.

nodes.name: wake.go
nodes.summary: Wake mechanisms for notifying a paired agent that new work is pending, via CLI exec, HTTP API, or MCP notification file.

nodes.name: wakeAPI
nodes.summary: Posts a wake payload to a configured HTTP endpoint with optional bearer auth, surfacing non-2xx responses as errors.

nodes.name: wakeMCP
nodes.summary: Writes a wake notification file into the MCP inbox so the receiving agent observes the event on its next poll.

nodes.name: resolveWakeAuth
nodes.summary: Resolves the wake API auth token from explicit config or env fallback (SIRSI_WAKE_TOKEN).

nodes.name: wake_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests all wake transports (CLI, API, MCP) for success and failure modes, plus auth resolution and mechanism selection.

nodes.name: workitem.go
nodes.summary: Persistent work queue with item lifecycle (pending, in-flight, done) and attempt history used by the runner executor.

nodes.name: WorkItem
nodes.summary: Record for a queued router task with agent assignment, status, document reference, and timing fields.

nodes.name: WorkQueue
nodes.summary: Container around a slice of WorkItems exposing add/find/status-transition and save helpers.

nodes.name: LoadWorkQueue
nodes.summary: Reads the work queue JSON file from disk, returning an empty queue when no file exists.

nodes.name: AddItem
nodes.summary: Appends a new WorkItem to the queue, deduplicating against existing pending items with the same key.

nodes.name: UpdateStatus
nodes.summary: Transitions a work item to a new status, validating the transition and stamping the updated time.

nodes.name: RecordAttempt
nodes.summary: Appends an Attempt record to a work item capturing dispatcher outcome and elapsed time.

nodes.name: workitem_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for work queue add/pending/save/load and status-transition behavior, plus inbox state migration semantics.

nodes.name: ansi.go
nodes.summary: Single-purpose helper that strips ANSI escape sequences from tool output before filtering.

nodes.name: StripANSI
nodes.summary: Removes ANSI control sequences from a string using a precompiled regex.

nodes.name: dedup.go
nodes.summary: Bounded ring buffer that deduplicates recently seen lines via a rolling hash window.

nodes.name: rtk.go
nodes.summary: RTK output filter: strips ANSI, deduplicates lines, collapses blanks, and truncates with head/tail preservation to shrink tool output before it hits AI context.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the Horus RTK 'Sieve' per ADR-015 — designed to reduce noise before output enters AI context.

nodes.name: FilterConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration controlling RTK filter knobs: max bytes, max lines, dedup window, tail preservation, and blank collapse.

nodes.name: FilterResult
nodes.summary: Result of a filter run including filtered bytes, dropped line count, and pre/post size ratio.

nodes.name: Filter
nodes.summary: Stateful filter instance binding a FilterConfig to a dedup ring used across Apply calls.

nodes.name: Apply
nodes.summary: Main filtering pipeline: strips ANSI, dedups via ring buffer, collapses blanks, and truncates with head/tail preservation when limits are exceeded.

nodes.name: DefaultConfig
nodes.summary: Returns canonical default RTK config values used by callers that do not supply explicit tuning.

nodes.name: truncateWithTail
nodes.summary: Truncates a slice of lines while preserving a configurable tail count so the most recent output remains visible.

nodes.name: rtk_test.go
nodes.summary: Extensive unit tests for RTK covering ANSI stripping, dedup ring edge cases, filter ratios, truncation behavior, and large-input handling.

nodes.name: work.go
nodes.summary: Filesystem-backed work item store with YAML frontmatter that lists, opens, and closes items in an inbox-style directory layout.

nodes.name: Item
nodes.summary: Work item record with from/to/title/status fields plus optional result metadata loaded from a markdown file's frontmatter.

nodes.name: Send
nodes.summary: Creates a new open work item file with YAML frontmatter under the items directory, requiring from and to fields.

nodes.name: ListInbox
nodes.summary: Returns open items addressed to the given recipient, sorted by creation time with hidden items filtered out.

nodes.name: ListAll
nodes.summary: Returns every persisted work item regardless of state, used for audit and admin tools.

nodes.name: Close
nodes.summary: Closes an open work item by attaching a result and flipping its status, refusing to double-close.

nodes.name: parse
nodes.summary: Parses a work item file's YAML frontmatter into an Item, handling YAML-sensitive titles via custom quote/unquote helpers.

nodes.name: work_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for the work store covering send validation, inbox listing, close semantics, frontmatter escaping, and back-compat parsing.

nodes.name: rtk.go
nodes.summary: Mobile-friendly wrapper around the internal RTK filter exposing RtkFilter and RtkDefaultConfig for gomobile bindings.
nodes.languageNotes: Designed for gomobile — exports flat, value-type APIs without channels or interface returns.

nodes.name: RtkFilter
nodes.summary: gomobile-compatible entry that filters a string using RTK with explicit config knobs and returns filtered text plus stats.

nodes.name: RtkDefaultConfig
nodes.summary: Returns the RTK default config knobs as primitive int/bool values so mobile callers can render UI defaults.

nodes.name: MainActivity.kt
nodes.summary: Android entry point activity that hosts Jetpack Compose navigation and routes to all deity-themed screens (Anubis, Brain, Horus, Ka, Seba, Stele, Thoth).
nodes.languageNotes: Jetpack Compose single-Activity pattern with NavHost routing.

nodes.name: PantheonBridge.kt
nodes.summary: JNI/IPC bridge that exposes the Pantheon Go core (scan, clean, deity operations) to the Android UI layer.

nodes.name: DeityCard.kt
nodes.summary: Reusable Compose card component rendering a deity tile with glyph, name, and tap action used on the Home screen.

nodes.name: AnubisScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Anubis screen for invoking infrastructure-cleaning scan rules and surfacing findings on Android via the Pantheon bridge.

nodes.name: BrainScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Brain (LLM/AI) screen for interacting with the Pantheon reasoning layer from the Android app via the native bridge.

nodes.name: HomeScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Top-level dashboard listing all deity entry tiles via DeityCard and routing to each deity's screen.

nodes.name: HorusScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Horus workstation-overview screen displaying the local code graph, file watcher status, and dashboard metrics on Android.

nodes.name: KaScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Ka (Ghost Hunter) screen for detecting and surfacing dead-app remnants and residual hauntings on the device.

nodes.name: SebaScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Seba (Star/Guide) screen surfacing teaching prompts and walkthroughs sourced from the Pantheon core.

nodes.name: SteleScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Stele screen rendering canonical documentation, ADRs, and versioned reference content fetched through the Pantheon bridge.

nodes.name: ThothScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Thoth memory/journal screen — the largest screen, providing the project's persistent knowledge interface (memory.yaml, journal entries) on Android.

nodes.name: main.go
nodes.summary: Entry point for the legacy `anubis` binary; initializes logging, wires Cobra root command, and executes with verbose/json flag support.
nodes.languageNotes: Thin main wrapper delegating to cmd package — typical Go cobra layout.

nodes.name: main.go
nodes.summary: Entry point for the `guard` standalone binary that delegates to the Cobra root command after initializing logging.

nodes.name: main.go
nodes.summary: Entry point for the `maat` QA-sovereign binary; standard Cobra bootstrap with logging initialization and global verbose/json flags.

nodes.name: main.go
nodes.summary: Entry point for the `scarab` fleet-sweep binary; bootstraps logging and dispatches to the Scarab Cobra command tree.

nodes.name: apps.go
nodes.summary: Ka module core: enumerates installed macOS applications from system_profiler, Homebrew casks, and Application directories, then scans Launch Services ghost registrations to detect residual app remnants.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses goroutines + channels to parallelize three enumeration sources, and shells out to system_profiler, brew, mdls, and lsregister for OS-native data.

nodes.name: InstalledApp
nodes.summary: Struct representing an installed macOS application with metadata (path, version, bundle ID, size, last-used time) and ghost-residue counters.

nodes.name: GhostRegistration
nodes.summary: Struct holding a single Launch Services ghost registration — bundle ID, path, and display name — extracted from `lsregister -dump`.

nodes.name: EnumerateApps
nodes.summary: Top-level Ka entry point: concurrently enumerates apps from system_profiler, Homebrew, and /Applications dirs, deduplicates, scans for ghosts, and attaches ghost counts/sizes to each installed app.

nodes.name: enumerateSystemProfiler
nodes.summary: Shells out to `system_profiler SPApplicationsDataType -json` and parses the result into the internal app list.

nodes.name: enumerateHomebrew
nodes.summary: Lists installed Homebrew casks via `brew list --cask` and maps each cask to its installed .app path with version info.

nodes.name: enumerateAppDirs
nodes.summary: Walks `/Applications`, `/Applications/Utilities`, and `~/Applications` to discover .app bundles directly from the filesystem.

nodes.name: enrichApp
nodes.summary: Enriches an InstalledApp with bundle ID, version, running status, on-disk size, last-used time, and ghost-residue counts by joining with the ghost index.

nodes.name: ScanLaunchServicesGhosts
nodes.summary: Streams `lsregister -dump` output to identify Launch Services bundle registrations whose paths no longer exist on disk — the ghost remnants Ka hunts.

nodes.name: ghostBelongsToInstalledApp
nodes.summary: Determines whether a ghost registration corresponds to an installed app using bundle-ID matching, prefix matching, and normalized-name heuristics.

nodes.name: appSize
nodes.summary: Computes on-disk size of a .app bundle by parsing `du -sk` output.

nodes.name: appLastUsed
nodes.summary: Reads the last-used timestamp of an app via `mdls -name kMDItemLastUsedDate`.

nodes.name: brewCaskVersion
nodes.summary: Queries `brew info --cask --json` to extract the installed version string for a given cask.

nodes.name: normalizeAppName
nodes.summary: Lowercases an app name and strips spaces, hyphens, and dots for fuzzy matching across enumeration sources.

nodes.name: darwin.go
nodes.summary: Darwin (macOS) platform provider for the generic Ka scanner — locates residual directories, builds an installed-app index from /Applications, and scans Launch Services registrations.

nodes.name: DarwinProvider
nodes.summary: Implements the Ka platform-provider interface for macOS, exposing methods to resolve residual locations, build installed indices, and scan the LS registry.

nodes.name: BuildInstalledIndex
nodes.summary: Builds a map of installed .app bundle names/IDs by reading every .app directory under /Applications and resolving bundle identifiers.

nodes.name: ScanRegistry
nodes.summary: Streams Launch Services registry dump output, parses bundle/path entries, and yields stale registrations that no longer exist on disk.

nodes.name: linux.go
nodes.summary: Linux platform provider for the Ka scanner — reads XDG .desktop application files and dpkg package index to identify installed apps and detect orphan desktop entries.

nodes.name: LinuxProvider
nodes.summary: Linux implementation of the Ka platform provider, reading .desktop files from XDG paths and consulting dpkg for package metadata.

nodes.name: BuildInstalledIndex
nodes.summary: Iterates XDG application directories, parses each .desktop file's Name field, and (where available) cross-references the dpkg index.

nodes.name: ScanRegistry
nodes.summary: Scans user XDG application directories for .desktop entries whose Exec target no longer exists — Linux equivalent of ghost detection.

nodes.name: indexDpkg
nodes.summary: Runs `dpkg-query -W` and populates the installed package set used for resolving .desktop entries to packages.

nodes.name: IsSystemID
nodes.summary: Classifies a desktop-entry app ID as belonging to a system component (GNOME, KDE, FreeDesktop, snap helpers) to be excluded from cleanup.

nodes.name: logging.go
nodes.summary: Project-wide structured logger built on `log/slog`; exposes Init/Debug/Info/Warn/Error/With helpers and supports verbose, quiet, and JSON modes.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard library slog wired through a package-level Logger pointer for global access.

nodes.name: Init
nodes.summary: Configures the global slog logger with verbose/quiet level overrides and optional JSON output.

nodes.name: logging_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the logging package verifying default level, verbose mode, quiet mode, error escalation, and context-attachment via With().

nodes.name: scanner.go
nodes.summary: Mirror module duplicate-file scanner: walks input paths, groups by size, partial-hashes likely matches, then full-SHA256-hashes confirmed groups in parallel to produce ranked duplicate sets.
nodes.languageNotes: Three-stage pipeline (size → partial hash → full SHA-256) with goroutine fan-out gated by a semaphore channel.

nodes.name: Scan
nodes.summary: Entry point that walks the requested roots, classifies files by media type, groups by size, then partial-hashes and full-hashes candidate groups concurrently to emit duplicate sets ordered by priority.

nodes.name: sortByPriority
nodes.summary: Stable-sorts a duplicate set so the canonical copy (shallowest path, oldest mtime) appears first and deletion candidates follow.

nodes.name: hashFilePartial
nodes.summary: Computes a SHA-256 over the first and last N bytes of a file as a cheap collision filter before doing full hashing.

nodes.name: hashFileSHA256
nodes.summary: Computes a full SHA-256 of a file via streaming io.Copy into the hasher.

nodes.name: FormatBytes
nodes.summary: Formats a byte count as a human-readable string with KiB/MiB/GiB units.

nodes.name: yield.go
nodes.summary: System-load yield checker: samples the OS load average, compares against CPU count, and advises callers to skip or warn about heavy work when the machine is busy.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dependency-injection (LoadProvider) so tests can stub system load without touching the OS, complying with Rule A16.

nodes.name: SystemLoad
nodes.summary: Snapshot of current system load — 1m and 5m averages, CPU count, computed ratio, and a verdict string used by callers to decide whether to defer work.

nodes.name: CheckWith
nodes.summary: Injectable variant of Check: samples load via the supplied provider and returns a SystemLoad with the load-ratio-derived verdict.

nodes.name: ShouldYield
nodes.summary: Returns true when the system is overloaded, logging a warning so callers can defer or skip heavy operations.

nodes.name: WarnIfHeavyWith
nodes.summary: Injectable variant of WarnIfHeavy: prints a styled CLI warning when load is high and the user has not passed --force.

nodes.name: getLoadAverageUnix
nodes.summary: Reads 1- and 5-minute load averages from `sysctl vm.loadavg` on Darwin or `/proc/loadavg` on Linux.

nodes.name: commands.ts
nodes.summary: Registers all VS Code command handlers for the Pantheon extension, wiring up scan, clean, renice, Thoth context, accountability, status, settings, and crashpad commands.
nodes.languageNotes: Single huge registerCommands function that hosts many vscode.commands.registerCommand callbacks; typical VS Code extension command-aggregator pattern.

nodes.name: registerCommands
nodes.summary: Registers every Pantheon VS Code command (scan, clean, renice, Thoth context, accountability report, status, settings, crashpad) and pushes their disposables onto the extension context.

nodes.name: handleCommandError
nodes.summary: Centralized error handler that classifies command failures (binary missing, permission denied) and surfaces appropriate VS Code messages plus output channel logs.

nodes.name: crashpadMonitor.ts
nodes.summary: Monitors VS Code's Crashpad pending-dumps directory, detects extension-host crashes, warns the user once per session, and exposes a webview report with cleanup actions.
nodes.languageNotes: Polls the filesystem on an interval; reads minidump headers via fs.openSync/readSync to classify crashes.

nodes.name: CrashpadMonitor
nodes.summary: Polls VS Code's Crashpad directory for pending minidumps, tracks crash history, updates a status bar indicator, and renders an HTML webview crash report.

nodes.name: diagnostics.ts
nodes.summary: Loads Pantheon scan findings from disk and surfaces them as VS Code diagnostics with associated quick-fix code actions.

nodes.name: PantheonDiagnostics
nodes.summary: Owns the diagnostic collection: refreshes from latest-scan JSON, maps Pantheon findings to vscode.Diagnostic entries scoped to relevant workspace paths.

nodes.name: PantheonCodeActionProvider
nodes.summary: Provides VS Code code actions for Pantheon diagnostics, exposing quick fixes that invoke the Pantheon binary for the offending file.

nodes.name: extension.ts
nodes.summary: VS Code extension entry point that activates Guardian, Thoth, accountability, crashpad, diagnostics, and status-bar subsystems and registers all commands.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard VS Code activate/deactivate pair; instantiates and disposes long-lived services via context.subscriptions.

nodes.name: activate
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the Pantheon extension: reads configuration, starts Guardian/Thoth/accountability/crashpad/diagnostics services, registers commands, and applies optimal workspace settings.

nodes.name: deactivate
nodes.summary: Disposes all Pantheon subsystems (Guardian, status bar, Thoth, accountability engine, crashpad monitor, diagnostics) on extension shutdown.

nodes.name: applyOptimalSettings
nodes.summary: Applies Pantheon's recommended VS Code workspace settings (file watcher excludes, search excludes, language-server memory caps) for healthier resource usage.

nodes.name: guardian.ts
nodes.summary: Background guardian that periodically renices runaway LSP/language-server processes and detects sustained memory pressure to trigger LSP restarts.
nodes.languageNotes: Spawns the Pantheon CLI binary via execFile; parses ps output to find LSP-pattern processes and applies renice/ionice.

nodes.name: Guardian
nodes.summary: Periodically scans for memory-hungry LSP processes, applies renice/ionice via the Pantheon binary, tracks memory-pressure history, and triggers LSP restarts when thresholds are sustained.

nodes.name: statusBar.ts
nodes.summary: VS Code status-bar indicator that polls Pantheon CLI for live LSP/system metrics and renders a tooltip with current pressure state.

nodes.name: PantheonStatusBar
nodes.summary: Maintains the Pantheon status-bar item: polls process metrics on an interval, classifies state (healthy/warning/critical), and builds a rich Markdown tooltip.

nodes.name: thothAccountability.ts
nodes.summary: Thoth accountability engine: benchmarks cold-start token savings from memory.yaml versus full source scans, computes dollar savings by model, persists lifetime stats, and renders a webview report.
nodes.languageNotes: Single 850-line class with persistent JSON state and per-session benchmarking against workspace source size.

nodes.name: ThothAccountabilityEngine
nodes.summary: Runs cold-start benchmarks comparing source-tree token cost vs. compressed memory.yaml, calculates per-session and lifetime dollar savings, validates freshness and coverage, and renders an HTML accountability report.

nodes.name: thothProvider.ts
nodes.summary: Loads and watches the workspace's .thoth/memory.yaml, parses it into structured context, and exposes compressed-context and summary helpers for other extension subsystems.

nodes.name: ThothProvider
nodes.summary: Loads .thoth/memory.yaml, watches it for changes, parses workstreams and recent changes, and exposes accessors for summary text, compressed context, and load state.

nodes.name: chromeProfilesProvider.ts
nodes.summary: VS Code TreeDataProvider that discovers and lists local Chrome profiles by reading Chrome's Local State JSON across macOS, Linux, and Windows.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements vscode.TreeDataProvider with EventEmitter-based refresh.

nodes.name: ChromeProfilesProvider
nodes.summary: TreeDataProvider implementation that enumerates Chrome profiles by scanning the OS-specific Chrome user data directory.

nodes.name: ChromeProfileNode
nodes.summary: TreeItem subclass representing a single Chrome profile entry in the sidebar.

nodes.name: commands.ts
nodes.summary: Registers all Seshat VS Code commands (open KI, sync to/from Gemini, create KI, set Chrome profile, show status) and spawns the pantheon CLI binary.

nodes.name: registerCommands
nodes.summary: Wires Seshat commands into the VS Code command palette, connecting tree providers, the dashboard, and the pantheon binary.

nodes.name: runPantheonCommand
nodes.summary: Spawns the pantheon CLI as a child process, captures stdout/stderr, and resolves with the trimmed output or rejects on non-zero exit.

nodes.name: dashboard.ts
nodes.summary: Seshat knowledge dashboard implemented as a VS Code webview panel that renders KI metrics, conversation counts, and an interactive HTML view.
nodes.languageNotes: Generates HTML/CSS/JS inline in TypeScript template literals; escapes user content via escapeHtml.

nodes.name: SeshatDashboard
nodes.summary: Manages the Seshat webview panel lifecycle, gathers knowledge data from disk, and renders the dashboard HTML with KI tables and stats.

nodes.name: extension.ts
nodes.summary: VS Code extension entry point that activates Seshat: resolves paths, sets up status bar, registers tree providers and commands, and optionally auto-syncs on startup.

nodes.name: activate
nodes.summary: Activation hook that bootstraps the Seshat extension: creates output channel, status bar, tree providers, commands, and handles first-run welcome.

nodes.name: knowledgeProvider.ts
nodes.summary: VS Code TreeDataProvider that lists Knowledge Items (KIs) and their markdown artifacts from the Seshat knowledge directory.

nodes.name: KnowledgeItemsProvider
nodes.summary: TreeDataProvider that reads the knowledge directory, parses each KI's manifest.json, and exposes its artifacts as collapsible tree nodes.

nodes.name: KnowledgeItemNode
nodes.summary: TreeItem subclass representing a Knowledge Item or artifact in the Seshat sidebar.

nodes.name: paths.ts
nodes.summary: Resolves the canonical filesystem layout for Seshat (root, brain, knowledge, manifest) honoring an optional user-supplied override.

nodes.name: resolvePaths
nodes.summary: Returns a SeshatPaths object derived from an override directory or the default `~/.sirsi/seshat` layout.

nodes.name: syncStatusProvider.ts
nodes.summary: VS Code TreeDataProvider that displays the Seshat sync manifest status (bidirectional sync timestamps with relative time formatting).

nodes.name: SyncStatusProvider
nodes.summary: Reads the sync manifest JSON and surfaces upload/download status with human-friendly relative timestamps as tree items.

nodes.name: SyncStatusNode
nodes.summary: TreeItem subclass representing a single sync direction status entry.

nodes.name: stats.go
nodes.summary: Collects a snapshot of system stats (RAM, git, accelerator, deity state, Ra scope status) and formats them as menubar items for the macOS menubar app.
nodes.languageNotes: Shells out to ps and reads JSON state files; combines vitals + deity registry into menubar-ready strings.

nodes.name: StatsSnapshot
nodes.summary: Composite snapshot struct holding RAM, git, Osiris risk, accelerator, deity, Ra scope, and timing fields for one menubar refresh cycle.

nodes.name: CollectStats
nodes.summary: Top-level collector that calls vitals.Collect plus collectDeities and collectRa to assemble a StatsSnapshot, measuring elapsed collection time.

nodes.name: collectDeities
nodes.summary: Scans running processes via `ps` and merges deity state from disk to count active deities and produce display strings.

nodes.name: collectRa
nodes.summary: Reads Ra deployment manifests and per-scope state/pid files to determine which Ra scopes are deployed and alive.

nodes.name: FormatMenuItems
nodes.summary: Formats a StatsSnapshot into the ordered list of menubar menu item strings shown in the dropdown.

nodes.name: registry.go
nodes.summary: Static registry of Pantheon deity metadata (key, glyph, name, role) with Lookup/Display/Keys helpers.

nodes.name: Lookup
nodes.summary: Looks up deity Info by key, returning an empty Info if no match exists.

nodes.name: state.go
nodes.summary: Persists per-deity runtime state (last used, last command, summaries, recommendations) to a JSON file under the user home directory.

nodes.name: PersistedState
nodes.summary: Serialized record of a deity's session state, including last command, summary, and recommendations.

nodes.name: LoadState
nodes.summary: Reads and decodes the deity state JSON from disk, returning an empty map when the file is absent.

nodes.name: SaveState
nodes.summary: Marshals deity state to indented JSON and writes it atomically to the home-directory state path, creating parent directories as needed.

nodes.name: state_test.go
nodes.summary: Test verifying SaveState/LoadState round-trip preserves session summary fields under a temporary HOME.

nodes.name: TestSaveLoadStatePreservesSessionSummary
nodes.summary: Round-trips a PersistedState through SaveState and LoadState in a t.TempDir HOME and asserts every field is preserved.

nodes.name: vitals.go
nodes.summary: macOS system telemetry collector that shells out to sysctl, vm_stat, top, ps, netstat, df, and git to populate a vitals Snapshot.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of exec.Command against macOS-specific tooling (sysctl, vm_stat, sw_vers); uses a mutex-guarded module-level net counter state.

nodes.name: Snapshot
nodes.summary: Comprehensive system snapshot with RAM, CPU, network, disk, uptime, git, accelerator, and machine-info fields.

nodes.name: Collect
nodes.summary: Entry point that runs every collectX helper in sequence and returns a fully populated Snapshot.

nodes.name: collectRAM
nodes.summary: Reads hw.memsize and parses vm_stat output to compute RAM total, used, percent, and pressure category.

nodes.name: collectGit
nodes.summary: Captures current git branch, uncommitted file count, and relative last-commit time from the working directory.

nodes.name: collectCPU
nodes.summary: Parses macOS top output for overall CPU percent and reads logical CPU count via sysctl.

nodes.name: collectTopProcs
nodes.summary: Runs `ps -Ao %cpu,rss,pid,comm -r` and extracts the top CPU-consuming processes as ProcInfo entries.

nodes.name: collectNetwork
nodes.summary: Parses netstat -ib byte counters and computes down/up bytes-per-second using a mutex-guarded previous-sample cache.

nodes.name: collectDisk
nodes.summary: Parses `df -g /` to compute used/total/free gigabytes and disk percent.

nodes.name: collectUptime
nodes.summary: Reads kern.boottime sysctl and formats the system uptime as a human-readable duration string.

nodes.name: collectMachineInfo
nodes.summary: Resolves hw.model to a friendly Apple Silicon model name and reads the macOS product version via sw_vers.

nodes.name: android.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow for building and testing the Android client of sirsi-pantheon on push and pull requests.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: Primary CI workflow enforcing the Rule A6 QA gate: lint, test, build, and binary size guard for the Go CLI and agent.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the canonical pre-merge checks (golangci-lint, go test, go build) referenced in PANTHEON_RULES.md §2.3.

nodes.name: claude.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow invoking Claude AI to respond to issue and PR mentions for AI-assisted code review.

nodes.name: deploy-docs.yml
nodes.summary: Deploys project documentation site to GitHub Pages on push to the main branch.

nodes.name: ios.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow building and testing the iOS/Swift client on macOS runners with Xcode.

nodes.name: release.yml
nodes.summary: Multi-platform release pipeline using goreleaser to publish sirsi CLI and sirsi-agent binaries on version tags per Rule A13.
nodes.languageNotes: Cross-compiles Go binaries for macOS, Linux, and Windows; injects version via -ldflags per the versioning protocol.

nodes.name: DESIGN.md
nodes.summary: Design document for the Ra Idea Router (Sirsi-wide agent hypervisor), defining the v0 filesystem-based router, message types (Proposal/Review/Decision), state file shape, agent protocol, and the mandatory workstream protocol that governs multi-agent collaboration.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary README for the Ra Idea Router multi-agent work queue, covering thread registration, the mandatory workstream protocol, parallel agent dispatch, router addressing law, and full automation via daemon and launch agent.

nodes.name: ROUTING.md
nodes.summary: Defines the Router Addressing Law, target agent selection rules, the state JSON shape, required artifact frontmatter, and the super-agent exception for cross-repo coordination.

nodes.name: agents.json
nodes.summary: Agent registry defining all participating router agents, their addresses, capabilities, and routing metadata used by the dispatcher to deliver pending items to the correct agent.

nodes.name: dispatch-ledger.json
nodes.summary: Append-only ledger of dispatch events recording which router items were delivered to which agents, supporting audit and idempotent re-dispatch behavior.

nodes.name: dispatch.sh
nodes.summary: Shell dispatcher script that reads pending router items from state.json and delivers them to the correct agent process, used by the wake plist and run-on-event hook for automated multi-agent relay.

nodes.name: run-on-event.sh
nodes.summary: Event-triggered wrapper that invokes dispatch.sh when filesystem or wake events fire, allowing the router to react to state changes without continuous polling.

nodes.name: state.json
nodes.summary: Live router state tracking active and completed topics, per-agent pending queues, last-read cursors for Claude and Codex, and the rules block that governs router behavior.

nodes.name: wake.example.plist
nodes.summary: Example macOS launchd plist that schedules periodic wake-and-dispatch runs, serving as the template users copy into ~/Library/LaunchAgents to enable full automation of the router.

nodes.name: 20260513-codex-product-reset.md
nodes.summary: Codex implementation brief for the Sirsi Pantheon product reset, identifying product-coherence, safety-trust, and usability gaps in TUI v0.21.0 and defining the target product direction.

nodes.name: 20260515-codex-idea-router-workstream-governance.md
nodes.summary: Governance decision for the idea-router workstream defining ownership, agent roles, and approval flow between codex, claude, and the user.

nodes.name: 20260516-execution-model.md
nodes.summary: Approved autonomous execution model for all SirsiMaster workstreams, codifying how codex and claude coordinate work without per-step user approval.

nodes.name: 20260518-claude-agent-router-prompt.md
nodes.summary: Canonical Claude-side agent router prompt specifying how Claude consumes, acknowledges, and responds to idea-router messages.

nodes.name: 20260518-codex-eta-driven-router-checks.md
nodes.summary: Codex decision introducing ETA-driven router checks so the heartbeat worker polls inboxes on a schedule derived from declared ETAs.

nodes.name: 20260518-codex-net-goal-weaving-architecture-doctrine.md
nodes.summary: Architecture doctrine establishing 'net goal weaving' as the planning pattern that ties workstream goals into a single shared net before execution.

nodes.name: 20260518-codex-universal-router-responder.md
nodes.summary: Codex decision defining a universal router responder so any agent can reply through a common ack/decision/closure protocol.

nodes.name: 20260519-claude-pantheon-tui-refactor-closed.md
nodes.summary: Closure entry for the Pantheon TUI refactor workstream, recording the final state before the subsequent TUI elimination decision.

nodes.name: 20260520-claude-finalwishes-tier1-ga-canonized.md

layers.name: CLI Entrypoints
layers.description: Cobra-based binary entrypoints under cmd/ for sirsi, sirsi-agent, sirsi-menubar, thoth, anubis, guard, maat, and scarab CLIs.

layers.name: Core Services (Pantheon Packages)
layers.description: Egyptian-pantheon-named Go packages under internal/ that implement the application core: agents, governance, memory, routing, dashboards, and platform services.

layers.name: Mobile Bindings & Apps
layers.description: gomobile bridge under mobile/ plus the Swift iOS app and Kotlin Android app that wrap the Go core for native mobile experiences.

layers.name: Editor Extensions
layers.description: VS Code TypeScript extensions under extensions/ that bridge editors (e.g. Gemini) to the Pantheon core.

layers.name: Agent Work Queue
layers.description: Multi-agent idea-router threads, queues, and coordination artifacts under .agents/ and related agent metadata directories.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation, ADRs, case studies, user guides, and root-level project memory/governance docs (README, CHANGELOG, ROADMAP, AGENTS, CLAUDE, etc.).

layers.name: Infrastructure & CI/CD
layers.description: Makefile build pipelines, GitHub Actions workflows, packaging assets (Homebrew, Pantheon.app), shell scripts, and pre-built binaries that build, test, and deliver the product.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Project-level configuration files: Go modules, linter and release configs, Firebase settings, YAML configs under configs/, and tool/IDE settings.

layers.name: Testing
layers.description: End-to-end tests under tests/ and coverage outputs that validate the system across binaries.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what sirsi-pantheon actually is: a zero-config, zero-telemetry infrastructure intelligence CLI that scans, detects, and remediates issues from a single laptop up to a 256-node fleet, with 81 built-in rules. The README frames the Egyptian-pantheon naming convention you will see across the codebase (sirsi, anubis, maat, thoth, scarab, guard) and points to the primary `sirsi` binary as the user-facing entry point. Keep this mental model in mind: every internal package is a 'god' that owns one slice of the infrastructure-hygiene job.

tour.title: The sirsi CLI Entry Point
tour.description: `cmd/sirsi/main.go` is the primary user-facing binary. It wires a Cobra command tree (status, isis, network, guard, jackal, mcp, ra, ...) and orchestrates the platform, output, and logging subsystems. This single file is the seam between 'what the user types' and 'which internal package runs'. Everything else in the tour hangs off the subcommands registered here.
tour.languageLesson: Go CLIs commonly use spf13/cobra: a root *cobra.Command holds global flags, and child commands are attached with AddCommand. main.go stays tiny — its job is to build the tree and call rootCmd.Execute(), delegating real work to internal packages.

tour.title: Sibling Binaries — The Pantheon of Entry Points
tour.description: sirsi-pantheon ships multiple binaries, each a thin Cobra bootstrap that delegates to one internal package. `sirsi-agent` is the headless orchestrator-driven agent (JSON in, JSON out); `sirsi-menubar` is the macOS systray companion; `thoth`, `maat`, `anubis`, `guard`, and `scarab` are standalone command-line entrypoints for memory, QA governance, legacy agent, security guard, and fleet sweep respectively. Seeing them side by side shows the product's shape: one shared core, many specialized surfaces.

tour.title: Platform Abstraction — Where the Code Meets the Machine
tour.description: Before any rule can run, the CLI has to know what it's running on. `internal/platform` detects OS, hardware, and environment, with concrete implementations for macOS, Linux, and iOS plus a `mock` used in tests. This is the foundational layer the rest of the pantheon depends on; the README's claim of 'one laptop to a 256-node fleet' is only possible because everything above this layer talks through `platform.Platform` rather than calling syscalls directly.
tour.languageLesson: Go uses build tags and per-OS filenames (linux.go, darwin.go, ios.go) so the compiler picks the right implementation per target. A single `Platform` interface lets callers stay platform-agnostic while specialized files hold the OS-specific work.

tour.title: Jackal — The 81 Rules Engine
tour.description: Jackal is the heart of the 'scans, detects, remediates' promise. `engine.go` runs rules against a target, `analyze.go` interprets results, `advisory.go` formats remediation guidance, and `installer.go` handles fix-up actions. The rule set lives under `internal/jackal/rules/` (ai, base, git, ...) — this is literally where the README's '81 rules' count comes from. Notice how `types.go` defines the shared vocabulary (Finding, Severity, Rule) that Jackal exports to every other package.

tour.title: Isis & Guard — Lint, Network, and Safety
tour.description: Isis owns code-hygiene scans (lint, vet, coverage, canon checks) and is invoked by the `sirsi isis` subcommand. Guard handles the security-and-safety side: auditing (`audit.go`), the `doctor` health check, memory pressure, and an 'antigravity' supervisor that catches runaway processes. Both packages depend on Jackal's types and on the platform abstraction from Step 4 — they are siblings in the pantheon, each responsible for one axis of infrastructure hygiene.

tour.title: Thoth & Ma'at — Memory and Governance
tour.description: Thoth is the persistent-knowledge service: `init.go` bootstraps `.thoth/`, `sync.go` keeps memory.yaml in lockstep with reality, `compact.go` summarizes long contexts, and `journal.go` records decisions. Ma'at is the QA-sovereign governor that enforces the project's pipeline rules (`pipeline.go`) and emits proofs (`proof.go`) that gates were honored. Together they make the CLI auditable: Thoth remembers, Ma'at certifies. The standalone `thoth` and `maat` binaries from Step 3 each wrap exactly one of these packages.

tour.title: MCP Server — Exposing Pantheon to LLMs
tour.description: `internal/mcp` implements a Model Context Protocol server so external LLM agents can drive sirsi-pantheon as a tool provider. `server.go` is the protocol surface, `tools.go` exposes Jackal/Isis/Guard operations as MCP tools, and `resources.go` makes project files readable through the protocol. This is how Pantheon plugs into Claude Code, Codex, and the wider agent ecosystem — the same internal packages from Steps 5–7, now reachable over MCP.

tour.title: Horus Dashboard — The Operational View
tour.description: When you want to see what the scanners found, Horus serves a local dashboard. `server.go` runs the HTTP layer, `pages.go` and `modules.go` render the UI, `findings.go` adapts Jackal Findings into dashboard rows, and `events.go` streams runner activity in real time. This is the visualization layer that sits on top of every other internal package — it consumes their output rather than producing its own.

tour.title: Mobile Bindings — Same Core, Native Apps
tour.description: The `mobile/` directory exposes the Go core to iOS and Android via gomobile. Each file wraps one pantheon service for native consumption: `horus.go` (dashboard), `anubis.go` (agent), `brain.go` (knowledge), `vault.go` (secrets), `seba.go` and `seshat.go` (additional services). The Swift iOS app and Kotlin Android app sit on top of these bindings, giving Pantheon a phone-native surface without duplicating any business logic.
tour.languageLesson: gomobile generates Java/Objective-C bindings from a restricted Go API surface: only basic types, interfaces, and structs with exported methods cross the bridge. That constraint is why each mobile/*.go file is intentionally narrow — it's a translation layer, not domain logic.

tour.title: Editor Extensions — Pantheon Inside VS Code
tour.description: `extensions/vscode/` is a TypeScript VS Code extension that surfaces the same Pantheon capabilities inside an editor. `extension.ts` is the activation entry, `commands.ts` registers the palette commands, `diagnostics.ts` reports Jackal findings as editor squiggles, `guardian.ts` watches for unsafe edits, and `thothProvider.ts` hooks Thoth's memory into the editor sidebar. It's a fourth surface (after CLI, mobile, and dashboard) on the same core.

tour.title: The Idea Router — Multi-Agent Work Queue
tour.description: `.agents/idea-router/` is sirsi-pantheon's multi-agent coordination substrate. `DESIGN.md` defines the filesystem-based router protocol; `ROUTING.md` codifies the Router Addressing Law; `agents.json` registers participating agents (Claude, Codex, ...); `state.json` is the live queue; `dispatch-ledger.json` is the append-only audit log. This is how the project itself is built — multiple AI agents collaborating through structured messages — and it's also a product surface other Sirsi repos consume.

tour.title: Build & Release — Makefile and Goreleaser
tour.description: The top-level `Makefile` is the build orchestrator with 21 targets: it compiles every binary from Step 3, bundles the macOS .app, builds the DMG, cross-compiles the iOS framework and Android AAR, and runs the test suite. `.goreleaser.yaml` defines the cross-platform release pipeline (archives, checksums, Homebrew tap) and `.golangci.yml` enforces lint rules across all Go code. Together they convert the source tree from earlier steps into shippable artifacts.
tour.languageLesson: Makefile targets declare file dependencies, but `.PHONY` targets (like `build`, `test`) are commands, not files. Goreleaser then takes over for cross-compilation — one YAML config produces signed binaries for darwin/linux/windows on every tag push.

tour.title: CI/CD — How Every Commit Is Gated
tour.description: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` enforces the Rule A6 QA gate — lint, test, build, and a binary-size guard — on every push and PR. `release.yml` runs the goreleaser pipeline from Step 13 on version tags. `android.yml` and `ios.yml` build the mobile clients on dedicated runners, and `deploy-docs.yml` ships documentation to GitHub Pages. This is the closing arc of the tour: the same code you read in Steps 2–11 is what these pipelines validate and ship.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows use `on:` triggers (push, pull_request, tags), `jobs:` for parallelism, and `steps:` for sequential commands. Caching Go module downloads with actions/cache typically cuts CI time by 30–60% on subsequent runs.
